title: dufrenoya euryphylla
intro: Dufrenoya euryphylla, also known as the "Balsam Tree," is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dufrenoya euryphylla include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to act as an antimicrobial agent, protecting against infections. Dufrenoya euryphylla is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and can also be applied topically to the affected area as a balm or salve.
